title: Communauté de communes du Haut Pays du Montreuillois
summary: The Communauté de communes du Haut Pays du Montreuillois is a communauté de communes, an intercommunal structure, in the Pas-de-Calais department, in the Hauts-de-France region, northern France. It was created in January 2017 by the merger of the former communautés de communes Canton de Fruges et environs and Canton d'Hucqueliers et environs. Its area is 420.8 km2, and its population was 15,747 in 2018. Its seat is in Fruges.
text:
The Communauté de communes du Haut Pays du Montreuillois is a communauté de communes, an intercommunal structure, in the Pas-de-Calais department, in the Hauts-de-France region, northern France. It was created in January 2017 by the merger of the former communautés de communes Canton de Fruges et environs and Canton d'Hucqueliers et environs. Its area is 420.8 km2, and its population was 15,747 in 2018. Its seat is in Fruges.

Composition
The communauté de communes consists of the following 49 communes:
